Get your API key from console.anthropic.com/api-keys
Stored locally in browser. Never transmitted except to Anthropic API.

Controls phone-specific hints (e.g. "run Locate on desktop"). Auto-detect uses your browser/screen. Override if it guesses wrong.

🔄 GitHub Sync

Sync your data (customers, drafts, projects, master-list edits) between devices via a private GitHub repo. The app file and embedded documents are NOT synced.

Fine-grained token, scoped to this one repo, Contents: Read and write.
Stored only in this device's browser. Re-enter on each device.

App code sync (pushes/updates the whole app HTML across devices):

✓ Pulled + merged at 7/15/2026, 6:47:56 PM

🧠 Shop Learning (control-type proposals)

The app learns feature → control-type mappings from every plan you build and syncs them shop-wide. Seed it from your entire plan history in one pass so the 📌 and 🤖 proposals are sharp right away.

—

Scans every saved project's rows for a feature + chosen control type, records the mappings (newest plan wins on conflicts, existing corrections kept), and syncs them to GitHub so all desktops share them. Safe to re-run anytime.

Downloads a self-contained document: the proposal logic, the local-matcher rules, and the full learned-mapping table (with a sign-off block). Open it and print to PDF for a controlled/auditable record.

How it works:

  1. Upload ODS drawing image
  2. App sends image to Anthropic API for analysis
  3. Claude counts balloon numbers on drawing
  4. Data rows field auto-populated with count

CAMCO CP GENERATOR — CHANGELOG
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Rev-45 (June 22, 2026)
- Fixed Master List script error (apostrophes in C'bore/C'Bore/C'Sink
  were breaking innerHTML template literals)
- Switched all cell rendering to textContent (safe for special chars)
- Simplified Master List to 8 core columns
- Fixed duplicate closing brace syntax error

Rev-44 (June 22, 2026)
- Ctrl+V paste: ODS screenshots, Setup (Excel/PDF), Checksheet
- Mobile-first redesign: stacked menus, bigger buttons
- Menu scrollable on mobile
- App Instructions screen added
- Master List force-reinit if count != 55

Rev-43 (June 21, 2026)
- Menu redesigned as card-based dashboard grid
- New Control Plan button: GREEN (#3d9a4a)
- APP_REV in header and footer

Rev-42
- Setup Sheet & Checksheet zones with clipboard support

Rev-41
- Global Ctrl+V paste listener for all zones

Rev-40
- PASTE buttons use Clipboard API

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
QUICK START

1. New Control Plan — fill required fields (yellow)
2. ODS Drawing — Ctrl+V to paste screenshot
3. Setup Sheet — Ctrl+V to paste Excel or PDF
4. Checksheet — Ctrl+V to paste Excel or PDF
5. Generate — builds skeleton CP from balloons

KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS
- Ctrl+V: Paste to active zone
- Click VIEW: preview loaded files
- Click RELOAD: clear and reload

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DEVELOPMENT RULES — CLAUDE MUST FOLLOW BEFORE EVERY DELIVERY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. NEVER use innerHTML with interpolated data values.
   Use createElement + textContent for all cell rendering.
   Data may contain apostrophes, quotes, or special chars
   that will break template literals silently.

2. ALWAYS check field names match between:
   - initDefaultMasterList() data object keys
   - renderMasterList() property references
   Mismatched field names cause silent undefined errors.

3. ALWAYS run node --check after every JS change.
   Extract script with:
   python3 -c "import re; open('/tmp/c.js','w').write(
     re.findall(r'<script>(.*?)</script>',
     open('file.html').read(), re.DOTALL)[0])"
   Then: node --check /tmp/c.js

4. FIX ROOT CAUSE — no incremental patches.
   Read the actual data and render function together
   before writing any fix.

5. BUMP REV on every delivery. Rev-NN increments by 1
   on every change, no exceptions.

6. SYNC to /mnt/project/ after every output update.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📊 Control Types Master List — Rev-11
🔷 C# / Blazor Conversion Plan

1. What this is

This app is a single HTML file. The long-term plan is to rebuild it as a proper installed Windows program using Microsoft's C# programming language with "Blazor" (Microsoft's system for building app screens). This page records everything done so far, the decisions made, and exactly how the final conversion will happen — so none of it lives only in a chat history.

2. What we built and how we got there (July 4, 2026)

Step 1 — Feasibility question. We asked how hard converting this HTML app to C#/Blazor would be. Answer: it is a full rebuild, not a translation. The two big issues: (a) a standard Blazor web app cannot run as a simple double-click file — it must be "served" by a small program running on a computer; (b) most of this app's complexity is drawing/image editing (balloons, Make Clearer, AI Retype), which C# has no built-in way to do — that part stays as embedded JavaScript even in a C# app.

Step 2 — Starter version built. Claude generated a complete C# starter project ("CamcoCP") containing: the data structures for Control Plans and the Master List; automatic saving; the GitHub sync plumbing (written but not finished); the Anthropic AI call for text reading; a Control Plan Excel builder using a C# library called ClosedXML (important: unlike the current tools, ClosedXML does not destroy balloon drawings when saving Excel files); and five working screens — Home, New Control Plan, CP Builder with the Master-List dropdown auto-fill, Master List, and Settings. Some buttons (Make Clearer, AI Retype, balloons) are placeholders that show a "not built yet" message.

Step 3 — Getting it running on the shop desktop (the bumps, in order). ① The .NET SDK (Microsoft's free build toolkit) wasn't installed — installed via winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8. ② "dotnet not recognized" persisted — because already-open windows don't see newly installed programs; fixed by closing and reopening PowerShell/VS Code. ③ The project was retargeted from .NET 8 to .NET 6 at CAMCO's request (yellow "out of support" warnings appear — harmless). ④ First build failed with two red errors in the Excel-builder file — a coding mistake by Claude, fixed and re-delivered. ⑤ The fix didn't take at first because three near-identical copies of the project folder existed in Downloads and the wrong one kept being built — resolved by using the newest folder and deleting the extras. Lesson: keep exactly ONE copy of the project on disk. ⑥ Success: the app built and ran, reachable in the browser at https://localhost:5001 while VS Code's terminal stays open.

3. The decision (was "Lane A vs Lane B")

Two ways were considered: grow the C# version gradually alongside the HTML app, or finish the HTML app first and convert once at the end. Decision: finish HTML first, convert once at the end. Reasons: every feature added to both apps is work done twice; the current Claude → HTML → GitHub → phone workflow is fast and familiar; and the conversion is easiest when the design is final. Plan of record:

  • Now → ~60 days: all improvements happen in this HTML app only, exactly as today. The C# starter project sits untouched — it was a successful proof that the desktop can build and run C#.
  • At the end: the finished HTML app is converted to C#/Blazor one time. Every improvement made along the way comes along automatically because the conversion starts from the final product.

4. How the final conversion will work — the checklist

When the app is declared done, this is the process (a series of chat sessions over some days — proven workable on July 4):

  1. Freeze the HTML app. Pick the final CP-RevNNN.html. No more feature changes after this point.
  2. Choose the packaging (decide with Claude at that time): a website-style app served on a PC (what the starter is now), or a true installed Windows program (called ".NET MAUI Blazor Hybrid" — works offline, opens files directly; the same C# code is reused either way).
  3. Start a new chat with Claude, attach the final CP-RevNNN.html AND the C# starter zip, and say: "Convert this final HTML app to C#/Blazor per the conversion plan in the app."
  4. Claude ports the screens and logic section by section — forms, project storage, GitHub sync with tombstone deletes, Master List, customers, validation, the Instructions viewer — delivering updated project files after each session.
  5. The drawing/image tools travel as-is: the balloon editor, Make Clearer, and AI Retype are self-contained JavaScript engines inside this HTML file; they get lifted into the C# app's JavaScript helper file (wwwroot/js/canvasInterop.js) largely unchanged. This is normal and intended — not a shortcut.
  6. The Excel builder moves to ClosedXML so balloons and the embedded ODS / ENG DWG picture tabs are added natively — ending the current rebuild-and-reinject workaround.
  7. Your role each session: replace the changed files in the one project folder, press Ctrl+C in the VS Code terminal, run dotnet run --project CamcoCP again, refresh the browser, and screenshot any red errors for Claude to fix. Same loop as July 4.
  8. Acceptance test: build the 912478-M-P and 912993-M Control Plans in the C# app and compare the Excel output cell-for-cell against the HTML app's output. Match = conversion complete.
  9. Only then retire the HTML app (keep the final file archived in GitHub).

5. Where things live

  • This HTML app: the one working tool — phone + desktop, GitHub Push/Pull for data.
  • C# starter project: "CamcoCP-Blazor-Phase1-net6" folder in Downloads on the shop desktop (keep ONE copy), zip also downloadable from the July 4 chat. Runs with: open folder in VS Code → Terminal → New Terminal → dotnet run --project CamcoCP → open the https://localhost address it prints.
  • Data: lives in each browser separately; GitHub is the bridge between phone and desktop (Settings → Pull Data from GitHub on any fresh copy).
